400.q: What is the villain in the Halo series?

400.a: Punishing difficulty, deliberate stamina-based combat, and minimal hand-holding

400.q: What is The Last of Us about?
400.a: Joel escorts immune girl Ellie across post-apocalyptic USA overrun by a fungal zombie pandemic

400.q: What is a speedrun?
400.a: Completing a game as fast as possible — often using glitches and optimized routes

400.q: Who created The Legend of Zelda?
400.a: Shigeru Miyamoto and Takashi Tezuka — published by Nintendo in 1986

400.q: What is the story of Half-Life?
400.a: A physicist at Black Mesa fights aliens and government soldiers after a failed experiment

400.q: What is a roguelike game?
400.a: A game with procedurally generated levels and permadeath — like Hades, Spelunky

400.q: What is the significance of Doom (1993)?
400.a: It defined the FPS genre and popularised PC gaming — so popular it was used to test hardware performance

400.q: What is the Witcher series about?
400.a: Geralt of Rivia — a monster hunter navigating a morally complex medieval fantasy world

400.q: What is God of War (2018) about?
400.a: Kratos moves to Norse mythology — he raises his son Atreus while confronting Norse gods and his past

400.q: What is Elden Ring?
400.a: A FromSoftware open-world action RPG created with George R.R. Martin — released 2022

400.q: What is Among Us?
400.a: A social deduction game where crewmates must find imposters sabotaging their spaceship

400.q: What is the significance of Tetris?
400.a: One of the best-selling games ever — its simple block-stacking gameplay is almost universally understood

600.a: Gameplay arising organically from player interaction with systems — not scripted, created by player creativity

600.q: Who invented the first commercially successful home console?
600.a: Magnavox Odyssey (1972) — designed by Ralph Baer, the Father of Video Games

600.q: What is soulslike?
600.a: A subgenre with punishing difficulty and deliberate combat — originated by Demon's Souls (2009) by FromSoftware

600.q: What is ludonarrative dissonance?
600.a: The conflict between a game's story and gameplay — e.g. a caring hero who kills thousands in gameplay

600.q: What is the Video Game Crash of 1983?
600.a: The collapse of the North American market due to oversaturation — Nintendo revived the industry with the NES

600.q: What is the concept behind Shadow of the Colossus?
600.a: You hunt and kill 16 colossi — the game questions whether you are the hero or the villain

600.q: What was the Video Game crash of 1983 caused by?
600.a: Market oversaturation, too many low-quality games, and the collapse of consumer trust in home consoles

600.q: What is the narrative innovation of Undertale?
600.a: It remembers your choices even if you reset — manipulating game conventions to create meaning and guilt
